Conclusion: in the family chat, the speed of debunking will never catch up to the speed of fear.
en.editorNote: For the family-chat elder who forwards every 'electric rates are exploding' rumor before reading it
variations: The spread of an electric-bill rumor, like summer heat, breaks its own record right on schedule every year.
I spent ten minutes debunking it; the only three words the elders retained were 'power is expensive.'

items.sourceNote: 2026 離峰時段用電比較省 時間電價 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
為了用離峰電價，我把所有耗電的事都排到深夜：洗衣、烘碗、充電、洗澡熱水。
現在我家最熱鬧的時段是凌晨。
我省下了電費，也順便把自己的生理時鐘，改成了夜行性動物。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個為了省電真的把作息整個搬到半夜的省錢戰友
variations: 離峰電價救了我的荷包，毀了我的睡眠，這筆交易其實沒有比較划算。
為了便宜那幾塊錢，我把自己活成了一隻會繳電費的夜貓子。

en.content:
To catch off-peak rates, I moved everything power-hungry to the dead of night: laundry, the dish dryer, charging, the water heater.
Now the busiest hour in my home is 2 AM.
I saved on electricity and, as a bonus, rewired my body clock into a nocturnal animal.
en.editorNote: Send to the savings buddy who genuinely shifted their whole schedule to the middle of the night for cheaper power
variations: Off-peak rates saved my wallet and destroyed my sleep — honestly not the better deal.
To save a few bucks, I've turned myself into a night owl that pays its own electric bill.

items.sourceNote: 一家4口兩個月電費近兩萬 朋友嚇爛 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友傳訊：「我家四口，兩個月電費快兩萬。」
我盯著螢幕，第一個反應不是同情，是換算：「兩萬，等於我半個月房租，等於十二趟外送，等於一張我捨不得買的演唱會門票。」
結論：別人的電費帳單，是我衡量自己貧窮等級的標準單位。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛收到天價帳單、在群組哀號的朋友，陪他一起換算成具體的痛
variations: 我已經不用「貴不貴」來形容電費了，我用「這可以換成幾頓飯」。
夏天的電費單，是一種會自動把你所有願望換算成度數的東西。

en.content:
A friend texts: 'Four people in my place, the two-month bill is almost six hundred bucks.'
I stare at the screen, and my first reaction isn't sympathy — it's conversion: 'Six hundred equals half my rent, equals twelve food deliveries, equals one concert ticket I've been too scared to buy.'
Conclusion: other people's electric bills are my standard unit for measuring my own poverty.
en.editorNote: For the friend wailing in the group chat over a sky-high bill — convert it into concrete pain together
variations: I've stopped describing the bill as 'expensive.' I describe it in 'how many meals is that.'
The summer bill is a thing that automatically converts all your dreams into kilowatt-hours.

items.sourceNote: Dcard 大家今年夏天電費多少 這樣正常嗎 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
每到夏天，網路上就會出現一種固定句型：「大家電費都多少？我這樣正常嗎？」
底下留言分成兩派：一派比你低，讓你懷疑人生；一派比你高，讓你瞬間安心。
結論：我們問的不是電費，是想找個人陪我們一起被台電傷害。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個剛在 Dcard 發文問「這樣正常嗎」、然後狂刷留言的朋友
variations: 「我這樣正常嗎」這句話，問的從來不是數字，是想要一點集體取暖。
夏天最療癒的事，是看到有人電費比我還高。

en.content:
Every summer, the internet spawns the same sentence: 'How much is everyone's bill? Is mine normal?'
The replies split into two camps: those lower than yours, who make you question your life; and those higher, who instantly make you feel okay.
Conclusion: we're not asking about the bill. We're looking for someone to be wounded by the power company alongside us.
en.editorNote: For the friend who just posted 'is this normal?' online and is now refreshing the replies
variations: 'Is this normal?' was never a question about the number — it's a request for collective comfort.
The most healing thing in summer is seeing someone whose bill is even higher than mine.

items.sourceNote: 夏月電價上路 帳單變貴真相 不是漲價是你用更多 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電出面解釋：「帳單變貴，不全是漲價，是你夏天用得更多。」
這句話聽起來像安慰，其實是補刀。
意思是：貴的不是電價，是我那顆一熱就想開冷氣、一開就不想關的軟弱意志。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個看到帳單想罵台電、結果被官方說「是你自己用太多」的朋友
variations: 台電：「不是我們漲價，是你變了。」這句話用在感情上也通。
原來最貴的不是夏月電價，是我對涼快的依賴。

en.content:
The power company steps out to explain: 'Your bill went up not just from the rate hike — you simply use more in summer.'
It sounds like reassurance. It's actually a second stab.
Translation: the expensive thing isn't the rate. It's my weak-willed heart that craves the AC the moment it's hot, and refuses to turn it off once it's on.
en.editorNote: For the friend who wanted to curse the power company over the bill, only to be told 'you just used too much'
variations: Power company: 'We didn't raise the price — you changed.' Works for breakups too.
Turns out the priciest thing isn't the summer rate. It's my dependence on being cool.

items.sourceNote: 變頻冷氣不關更省 台電認證關最省時間點 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我一直以為「變頻冷氣不關比較省」是真理，奉行了整個夏天。
後來看到台電認證：出門超過一小時，其實關掉才省。
那一刻我才懂，我不是在省電，我是在用「聽說比較省」這四個字，替自己不想關冷氣的懶找台階。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個整個夏天死不關冷氣、嘴上掛著「變頻不關比較省」的朋友
variations: 所有「不關比較省」的家電迷思，本質都是懶惰的高級包裝。
省電的最大障礙，是那些剛好支持我繼續耍廢的偽科學。

en.content:
I always believed 'leaving inverter AC on saves more' was gospel, and lived by it all summer.
Then the power company confirmed: if you're out for more than an hour, turning it off actually saves.
That's when it hit me — I wasn't saving power. I was using the phrase 'I heard it saves more' as a polite excuse for my own refusal to hit the off button.
en.editorNote: For the friend who never turns off the AC all summer, forever muttering 'inverters save more if you leave them on'
variations: Every 'leaving it on saves more' appliance myth is really just laziness in premium packaging.
The biggest obstacle to saving power is the pseudo-science that happens to endorse my slacking.

items.sourceNote: 用電超過 331 度 夏季單價漲幅顯著 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
夏天用電的人生分水嶺，叫做 331 度。
超過那條線，每一度都變貴，所以月底我會像在玩拆彈遊戲，盯著電表小心翼翼地過日子。
「今天還能開冷氣嗎？」「再開兩小時會不會引爆？」
我這輩子最緊張的數學題，原來是我家電表。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個月底會每天看電表、把開冷氣當成限時挑戰的精算型室友
variations: 331 度，是台電在你夏天人生裡埋的一條地雷，踩到就漲價。
月底盯電表的緊張感，比期末考還真實，因為這次真的會扣到錢。

en.content:
The great summer dividing line is 331 kWh.
Cross it and every unit gets pricier, so by month's end I'm playing a bomb-defusal game, tiptoeing through life with one eye on the meter.
'Can I still run the AC today?' 'Will two more hours detonate it?'
The most nerve-wracking math problem of my life turns out to be my own electric meter.
en.editorNote: For the spreadsheet-minded roommate who checks the meter daily and treats running the AC like a timed challenge
variations: 331 kWh is the landmine the power company buried in your summer — step on it and the rate jumps.
Watching the meter at month's end is more tense than finals, because this time you actually lose money.

items.sourceNote: 住宅電費平均月增 446 元 其中 326 元來自用電量增加 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
新聞解釋：「電費月增 446 元，其中 326 元是你冷氣開太久，只有 120 元是電價漲。」
我：「所以你的意思是，這筆錢主要不是台電的錯，是我的錯。」
台電：「我們從沒這樣說，但你說得很對。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每年都怪台電漲價、但其實是自己捨不得關冷氣的朋友
variations: 原來電費變貴，七成是我自己手賤一直按開機，三成才是電價。
夏天最殘忍的真相：帳單上的數字，大部分是你自己親手養大的。

en.content:
News explains: 'Your bill rose by 446 — but 326 of that is you running the AC longer, only 120 is the rate hike.'
Me: 'So you're saying this is mostly not the power company's fault, it's mine.'
Power company: 'We never said that, but you're absolutely right.'
en.editorNote: For the friend who blames the utility every year when it's really their own refusal to hit the off button
variations: Turns out 70% of my pricier bill is my own twitchy finger hitting 'on', and only 30% is the rate.
The cruelest summer truth: most of the number on that bill is something you raised by hand.

items.sourceNote: standing directly in front of the AC vent meme register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
下班回到家的第一件事，不是放包包，不是喝水，
是站在冷氣出風口正下方，把上衣領口拉開，讓冷風灌進去，閉上眼睛，像在做某種神聖的儀式。
那十秒鐘，是我這天唯一覺得有付電費值得的時刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一回家就衝去冷氣出風口下面站著充電的朋友
variations: 冷氣出風口下面那塊地板，是我家風水最好的位置。
夏天的幸福很簡單：出風口、領口、十秒鐘，剩下的我自己想辦法。

en.content:
First thing I do walking in after work isn't dropping my bag or grabbing water —
it's standing directly under the AC vent, pulling my collar open, letting the cold pour in, eyes closed, like some sacred ritual.
Those ten seconds are the only moment all day the electric bill feels worth it.
en.editorNote: For the friend who sprints to the vent to recharge the second they get home
variations: The patch of floor under the AC vent is the best real estate in my entire apartment.
Summer happiness is simple: a vent, an open collar, ten seconds. I'll figure out the rest myself.

items.sourceNote: 變頻冷氣不關更省 是迷思 累進電價跟時段無關 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我一直相信「變頻冷氣別關比較省」，直到看到專家說：累進電價只看你總共用幾度，跟你什麼時候開、關不關沒關係。
等於我這三年來那套精心設計的「不關冷氣省電法」，
從頭到尾都只是我替自己亂吹冷氣找的高級藉口。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個堅持冷氣 24 小時不關、還理直氣壯說「這樣才省」的室友
variations: 我以為我在省電，其實我只是發明了一個聽起來很科學的浪費理由。
變頻冷氣最省電的開法，是把那套「不關比較省」的信仰先關掉。

en.content:
I spent years believing 'never turn off an inverter AC, it saves more' — until an expert pointed out: tiered billing only counts total units used, not when you run it or whether you switch it off.
Which means my carefully engineered 'never-turn-it-off savings method' of the last three years
was, from start to finish, just a fancy excuse I invented for cranking the AC.
en.editorNote: For the roommate who insists on running the AC 24/7 and swears it's the frugal choice
variations: I thought I was saving power. I'd actually invented a scientific-sounding excuse to waste it.
The most energy-efficient way to run an inverter AC is to first switch off the belief that 'never off saves more'.

items.sourceNote: 夏月電價上路 帳單變貴真相 冷氣設 26 度 循環扇 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電：「冷氣 26 度搭循環扇，冷房更快更省。」
我買了循環扇。
現在我房間有冷氣的涼、循環扇的風、還有我看著兩張發票時的冷汗。
三股氣流，完美交織。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個為了省電而瘋狂加購各種小家電、結果愈買愈花錢的朋友
variations: 省電神器的共通點：買的時候很省，加進電費才知道不省。
循環扇真的有讓房間更涼，涼到我都忘了它也是要插電的。

en.content:
Power company: 'Set the AC to 78°F and add a circulator fan — faster cooling, more savings.'
So I bought a circulator fan.
Now my room has the AC's chill, the fan's breeze, and a cold sweat from staring at two receipts.
Three air currents, perfectly intertwined.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps buying gadgets to save power and somehow spends more each time
variations: Every 'energy-saving gadget' shares one trait: cheap to buy, not so cheap once it joins the bill.
The circulator fan really did cool the room down — so much I forgot it also needs to be plugged in.

items.sourceNote: complaining about heat then freezing when AC finally works meme register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
白天的我：「熱死了，誰受得了，我願意付任何電費換一點涼。」
冷氣開了二十分鐘後的我：「有點冷欸，把溫度調高一點好了，順便省電。」
人類的記性，永遠撐不過一台冷氣的暖機時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個前一秒喊熱、下一秒就喊冷去拿外套的善變朋友
variations: 我對熱的承諾，總是在冷氣生效的那一刻被我自己背叛。
夏天最大的矛盾，是熱的時候願意賣腎，涼的時候捨不得那幾塊電費。

en.content:
Daytime me: 'It's unbearable, I'd pay any electric bill for a little cool air.'
Me twenty minutes after the AC kicks in: 'Kind of chilly actually, let me bump the temp up — saves power too.'
Human memory never outlasts an AC's warm-up time.
en.editorNote: For the fickle friend who screams it's hot one second and grabs a sweater the next
variations: Every promise I make while hot gets betrayed by me the moment the AC starts working.
Summer's biggest contradiction: I'd sell a kidney when it's hot, then begrudge a few cents of power once it's cool.

items.sourceNote: 「電費太高可能要把房子賣了」meme register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這個月的電費單寄到，我認真坐下來算了一下：
與其每個月被冷氣電費凌遲，不如直接把房子賣掉，搬去百貨公司住。
至少那裡的冷氣，不用我付。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個拆完帳單後說「乾脆搬去 IKEA 樣品屋住」的朋友
variations: 我家最值錢的資產不是房子，是房子裡那台願意為我運轉的冷氣。
夏天的人生規劃：賣房，退租，搬進一個別人付電費的地方。

en.content:
This month's bill landed, and I sat down to do the math seriously:
instead of getting tortured by the AC bill every month, I might as well sell the house and move into a department store.
At least their AC isn't on my dime.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who, after opening the bill, declares they're moving into an IKEA showroom
variations: The most valuable asset in my home isn't the house — it's the AC willing to run for me.
Summer life plan: sell the house, break the lease, move somewhere someone else pays the power.

items.sourceNote: 站在冷氣出風口前 享受 meme register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
冷氣剛打開的前三分鐘，我會站在出風口正下方，閉上眼睛，雙手張開。
那不是在吹冷氣，那是一場儀式——
我在用全身，迎接今晚這幾度電的恩賜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個冷氣一開就立刻衝去站在出風口下的朋友，他在領聖體
variations: 出風口正下方那個位置，是我家夏天唯一的 VIP 席。
冷氣出風口之於夏天，等同暖爐之於冬天——人類永遠在追著溫度跑。

en.content:
For the first three minutes after the AC turns on, I stand directly under the vent, eyes closed, arms wide open.
That's not cooling off. That's a ceremony —
my whole body receiving the blessing of tonight's few units of power.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who sprints to stand under the vent the second the AC kicks on — they're taking communion
variations: The spot directly under the vent is the only VIP seat in my house all summer.
The AC vent is to summer what the heater is to winter — humans forever chasing a temperature.

items.sourceNote: 舒眠模式 半夜被熱醒 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
睡覺開舒眠模式，據說可以省電。
它的運作原理是：每隔一小時悄悄把溫度調高一度，溫柔到你不會發現。
直到凌晨四點，你在一灘汗裡醒來，才懂什麼叫做「被自己的省電設定背叛」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個堅持開舒眠模式、然後每天清晨四點準時被熱醒的朋友
variations: 舒眠模式的「舒」，是舒服的舒，也是它舒舒服服把溫度調高的舒。
省電與好眠，夏天的我永遠只能選一個，而我每次都選錯。

en.content:
Sleep mode on the AC supposedly saves power.
Here's how it works: every hour it quietly nudges the temperature up one degree, so gently you never notice.
Until 4 AM, when you wake up in a puddle of sweat and finally understand what it means to be betrayed by your own energy-saving setting.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who insists on sleep mode and wakes up drenched at 4 AM like clockwork
variations: Sleep mode lets the AC sleep comfortably while it cranks the temperature up — you're the one who doesn't.
Saving power or sleeping well: summer me can only pick one, and I pick wrong every single night.

items.sourceNote: 為什麼夏天電費貴 尖峰供電成本 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
台電說：「夏天大家都開冷氣，供電成本高，所以電價才貴。」
我聽完默默點頭，覺得很有道理。
然後我看了一下我的薪水，發現它沒有因為「大家夏天都很需要錢」而跟著漲。
供需法則，原來只對台電有效。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個聽完台電解釋後，理性上認同、情感上想哭的朋友
variations: 夏天什麼都因為「需求高」而漲價，只有我的薪水堅持走計畫經濟。
我完全理解夏天電價為什麼貴，理解到一邊流淚一邊繳費。

en.content:
The power company explains: 'Everyone runs the AC in summer, supply costs spike, so rates go up.'
I nod along quietly. Makes total sense.
Then I check my salary and notice it didn't rise just because 'everyone needs money more in summer.'
Turns out the law of supply and demand only applies to the power company.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who rationally agrees with the utility's explanation while emotionally wanting to cry
variations: Everything in summer goes up because 'demand is high' — only my salary insists on a planned economy.
I fully understand why summer rates are high. I understand it while paying the bill in tears.

items.sourceNote: 冷氣是人類最偉大發明 熱浪求生 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
熱浪來襲的這幾天，我終於想通了一件事：
人類史上最偉大的發明，不是火、不是輪子、不是網路，
是冷氣。
火讓我們活下來，冷氣讓我們在夏天「願意」活下來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在熱浪特報那天，對著冷氣外機默默感謝的朋友
variations: 我可以三天不出門，但不能三小時沒冷氣，這是我這個夏天認清的底線。
發明冷氣的人應該被立銅像，立在一個有冷氣的室內。

en.content:
After these few days of heatwave, I've finally figured something out:
the greatest invention in human history isn't fire, isn't the wheel, isn't the internet —
it's air conditioning.
Fire let us survive. AC is what makes us willing to survive summer.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who silently thanks the AC compressor on the day of a heat advisory
variations: I can go three days without leaving home, but not three hours without AC — that's the line I drew this summer.
Whoever invented air conditioning deserves a bronze statue, erected somewhere indoors with the AC on.
